mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly supporting higher S&P 500 valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline when FEDFUNDS is near zero.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment rates signal weak labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, creating a drag on corporate earnings and resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising housing starts and near-zero interest rates indicates a liquidity-fueled asset boom, where cheap credit drives demand into real estate and risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of high HOUST and low FEDFUNDS fails to predict positive returns (Rank IC < 0.05) in a multivariate regression controlling for other macro factors.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rebound in industrial production following a severe economic shock signals supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, driving positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods of rising INDPRO immediately following a macroeconomic shock.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects broad-based economic expansion and corporate revenue growth, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 shows positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total non-farm payrolls signals labor market tightening and rising wage pressures, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in > 60% of months where PAYEMS shows a positive month-over-month change.

mechanism_narrative: The co-occurrence of low inflation and low interest rates creates a 'Goldilocks' environment where central banks can maintain accommodative policy without triggering inflation fears, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) in predicting 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in real retail sales driven by fiscal stimulus measures signals an immediate boost to corporate earnings, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in > 50% of weeks where RSAFS shows a significant increase during periods of active fiscal stimulus.
